codeReview.summary: The fix directly resolves the prior blocking issue by gating Cache-Control injection to successful GET responses only, and adds middleware-level tests proving header presence for 2xx and absence for 4xx/5xx and POST. Compared against the original feature commit, the behavior now prevents stale negative cache states without altering path-based header policies for successful responses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-backend-worker skill requires `cargo build` in full validation, but the feature verification steps and this worker run only `cargo test`/`cargo check`; this mismatch creates ambiguous completion criteria.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-backend-worker/SKILL.md Work Procedure step 7 lists `cargo build`; handoff 2026-03-22T00-28-07-291Z__fix-cache-headers-status-gating__3679924d-5000-4694-8be2-c3a00476a944.json verification.commandsRun includes `cd backend && cargo test cache_headers`, `cd backend && cargo test`, and `cd backend && cargo check` only.

summary: PASS: reviewed transcript skeleton, original failure report, and both original/fix diffs. Commit 40a5f6a fully addresses the blocking status-gating defect identified in backend-cache-headers by restricting Cache-Control injection to 2xx GET responses and adding targeted regression tests, with no new blocking regressions found.
